name: Omsk Oblast (Russia)

colors.symbolism: the courage of the people and the historic banners of the Cossacks
purity and the openness of the Siberian plains
the Irtysh River, which flows through the oblast

description: Three vertical stripes of red, white and red of equal width; a blue wavy vertical band runs through the centre of the white stripe, representing the Irtysh River.
history: Adopted on 27 February 2003 by the Legislative Assembly of Omsk Oblast.

features.symbolism: red is the historic colour of the Russian banners of the Cossack hosts who founded Omsk; white stands for openness and the wide Siberian plains.
a blue wavy vertical band running through the white stripe, representing the Irtysh River — the geographic backbone of the oblast.
